scope_note: QA Fibonacci Inert phi-Slope Formula mod p^2 cert [431]. Closes a gap explicitly deferred by cert [421] (Hensel-lifted Binet mod p^2 was built for split primes only). Primary sources: Hensel, K. (1897) 'Ueber eine neue Begruendung der Theorie der algebraischen Zahlen' Jahresbericht der DMV 6 pp. 83-88 (Galois ring / Hensel lifting); Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) doi:10.2307/2309169 (Pisano periods, alpha(p)|p-(5/p)); Sun, Z.H. and Sun, Z.W. (1992) 'Fibonacci and Lucas congruences and their applications' Acta Arithmetica 61 pp. 119-129 (p^2-level Fibonacci structure, WSS definition, depth invariant delta(p)). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): construction R_p=(Z/p^2Z)[phi]/(phi^2-phi-1), the mod-p^2 lift of cert [424]'s F_{p^2}=(Z/pZ)[phi]/(phi^2-phi-1), for inert primes p (5/p)=-1; elements are pairs (a,b) for a+b*phi with a,b in Z/p^2Z; psi=1-phi; s=phi-psi=2*phi-1 satisfies s^2=5 (a unit mod p^2 since p!=5 inert), giving s^{-1}=5^{-1}*s without a separate Hensel lift of sqrt(5) (unlike the split case in [421], R_p is a free rank-2 module by construction). (C1) ring correctness: phi^2=phi+1, psi^2=psi+1, phi*psi=-1, phi+psi=1, s^2=5, s invertible, all mod p^2 in R_p; 20/20 inert primes <=200 PASS. (C2) Binet mod p^2: F_n=(phi^n-psi^n)*s^{-1} (mod p^2) in R_p for n=1..15; 225/225 cases (15 inert primes <=150) PASS. (C3) delta(p) agreement: delta(p) computed via R_p-Binet equals delta(p) via direct integer recurrence (same delta used in [429]/[430]); 47/47 inert primes <=500 PASS. (C4) WSS via Frobenius: delta(p)=0 iff phi^{alpha(p)}=psi^{alpha(p)} in R_p; 47/47 inert primes <=500 PASS, no WSS prime found among inert primes (consistent with [429]'s split+combined sweep). CONNECTS TO CHAIN: completes the split/inert pairing pattern established by [423]->[424] (Frobenius order, mod p) at depth p^2: [421] (split phi-slope mod p^2) + [431] (inert phi-slope mod p^2) together give the full Galois/Frobenius interpretation of delta(p) -- and hence the WSS criterion [429] -- for ALL primes. THEOREM NT: all pure integer; R_p pair arithmetic (ring_mul/ring_add/ring_sub/ring_pow) stays in Z/p^2Z throughout; no float, no observer layer. 4 checks PASS; self-test ok. Derived 2026-06-15.
